Beside him, the Man in Red Hoodie, in a red hoodie, uses a silver laptop and black microphone, smiling and gesturing as he recounts, "You guys remember the first generation of AirPods? I lived in San Francisco when that happened and all of a sudden everyone was walking around with these like these like antennas coming out of their ears and I was like, this looks so dumb. Oh my god. And I was laughing at people for months." The Man in Gray Jacket interjects with "Same" in agreement, and the Man in Red Hoodie concludes, "And then now it's just completely normal." The camera then cuts to a close-up of the Man in Green Hoodie, wearing a green hoodie, black over-ear headphones, and a green wristband, speaking expressively into a red microphone. He uses frequent hand gestures—pinching, open palms, and adjusting his headphones—as he elaborates on the future of media: "I do get asked a lot, what do I think is the future of media, like we've been doing this whole 2D YouTube video thing for a while, and the natural inclination is, yeah, the future would be an immersive video... I hold the phone in my hand and you can see it like it's in your hand and you can look around and that feels like that's the future we're barreling towards. I just don't know how far along that line we are yet. I also don't know if it's actually going to work out that way." The camera alternates between his close-up and wider shots of the first two men, who listen attentively, nodding. The Man in Gray Jacket, holding a red pen, raises it in a pointing gesture and asks, "MKB 3D?", to which the Man in Red Hoodie responds, "Well, so also HD still. So I used to to".
